project.name: dentalemon
project.description: Business domain / flow graph for the dentalemon dental clinic platform. Flows modeled as end-to-end FE↔BE user journeys (e.g. Start Visit = POST draft → PATCH active). Refreshed in UA_KG_UPGRADE U1.

nodes.name: Patient Management
nodes.summary: Owns the patient record as the central PII safeguard: registration, demographics, households, duplicate detection/merge, recalls, insurance, and communication consent. Every clinical and billing flow resolves a patient here first.

businessRules: PII is never logged in plain text
Consent (marketing/sms/email/data-sharing) validated before outbound communication
Duplicate patients are surfaced and merged, never silently created
Patients are branch-scoped via preferred_branch_id (RLS)
crossDomainInteractions: Resolved by clinical-workspace before any visit

nodes.name: Scheduling & Front Office
nodes.summary: Generic time-based scheduling specialized for the clinic: appointment booking, confirmation, check-in, cancellation, the chairside queue board, the waitlist, and patient-facing online booking. Drives the day's calendar and feeds patients into the clinical queue.

businessRules: Appointments must fall within configured working hours
Cancellation restricted to owner/staff_full roles (RLS-enforced)
Appointment FSM: scheduled -> confirmed -> checked-in / cancelled
Public online booking is rate-limited and slot-held before confirmation

nodes.name: Clinical Workspace
nodes.summary: The chairside clinical record: dental visits and their FSM, tooth-by-tooth dental charting, findings, treatments and treatment plans, periodontal exams, prescriptions, lab orders, consent capture, medical history, and the patient medical document (PMD). Local-first with offline conflict resolution.

businessRules: A patient may have only one open (draft/active) visit at a time
Visit FSM: draft -> active -> completed; draft->completed is invalid
Chart save merges against a baseline to avoid offline data loss (LWW + conflict capture)
Perio chart must be completed before staging is finalized
Findings convert into proposed treatments

nodes.name: Diagnostic Imaging
nodes.summary: Capture, storage and analysis of dental imaging: intraoral/FMX uploads, DICOM/CBCT studies via presigned multipart upload, imaging findings and measurements, and cephalometric tracing (landmarks, angle analysis, superimposition, signed reports).

businessRules: Large studies upload via presigned multipart PUT (S3/MinIO), bypassing the auth cookie
Ceph reports pin analysis_type/norm_version/formula_version for reproducibility
Imaging findings can link to clinical findings
crossDomainInteractions: Uses storage domain for binary persistence

nodes.name: Treatment Case Presentation
nodes.summary: Presents an accepted treatment plan to the patient as a signed legal artifact: assembles plan + imaging context, captures the e-signature acceptance or rejection, and exposes the accepted plan read-only for the legal record.

businessRules: Acceptance records signerName/decision/timestamp as an immutable artifact
A presentation decision is accepted or rejected, never silently changed

nodes.name: Billing & Revenue Cycle
nodes.summary: Invoice-based revenue cycle: build invoices from treatments, apply discounts, issue, record/void payments, generate receipts, payment plans, insurance claims with remittance, and accounts-receivable reporting (AR aging, collections, statements).

businessRules: Billing report endpoints must scope by branchId or leak cross-tenant money/PHI (EM-BIL-002)
Invoice FSM: draft -> issued; voids fail-closed
Payments and voids are auditable and reversible only via void, not delete
Patient balance derives from issued invoices minus payments

nodes.name: Organization & Access Control
nodes.summary: Tenant setup and security: organization/branch creation and activation, staff membership with PIN-based chairside auth, role-based permission grid, fee schedules, consent templates, and the dashboard summary. Establishes the multi-tenant, branch-scoped boundary all other domains enforce.

businessRules: Every record is tenant/branch scoped and enforced via Postgres RLS
Chairside actions require a verified PIN session
Permissions are role-based; sensitive actions (cancellation, billing) gate on role
Org must be activated before product features unlock
crossDomainInteractions: Provides auth/branch context to all domains
Fee schedules feed billing invoice pricing
Consent templates feed clinical consent capture

nodes.name: Patient Engagement & Communications
nodes.summary: Outbound and patient-facing engagement: multi-channel notifications (email + OneSignal push), transactional emails, NPS reviews, real-time chat/video comms, and the patient portal (appointments, bills, balance).

businessRules: Push uses external_id targeting against a single OneSignal app
Communication respects patient consent flags
Portal exposes only the patient's own appointments/invoices/balance
crossDomainInteractions: Notified by scheduling (appointment events) and billing (payment events)
Portal reads from scheduling and billing

nodes.name: Register / Update Patient
nodes.summary: Front office registers a new patient (or edits demographics), with duplicate detection surfacing potential matches before a new record is created.

nodes.name: Detect & Merge Duplicate Patients
nodes.summary: Surfaces candidate duplicate patients and lets staff merge them into a single record to preserve a single source of truth.

nodes.name: Schedule Appointment (Staff)
nodes.summary: Staff create an appointment from the calendar, validated against working hours and availability, then confirm it.

nodes.name: Check-In & Queue
nodes.summary: On arrival, staff check the patient in; the patient enters the chairside queue board where their status advances toward treatment.

nodes.name: Patient Online Booking
nodes.summary: Public patient self-booking wizard: pick service/provider, see live availability, hold a slot, enter details, and confirm.

nodes.name: Start Clinical Visit
nodes.summary: From the workspace, starting a visit is a frontend two-step: create a draft visit, then transition it to active so it is chartable and completable. The one-open-visit rule gates the New Visit affordance.

nodes.name: Chart Dentition & Record Findings
nodes.summary: Clinician charts teeth surface-by-surface, records findings, and converts findings into proposed treatments; chart saves merge against a baseline with offline conflict handling.

nodes.name: Periodontal Exam
nodes.summary: Clinician creates a perio chart, records per-tooth probing readings, and completes the chart to lock staging; longitudinal comparison shows trend vs prior exams.

nodes.name: Complete Visit
nodes.summary: Clinician runs the pre-completion checklist (medical history reviewed, consent captured), marks treatments done, then transitions the active visit to completed.

nodes.name: Capture & Upload Imaging
nodes.summary: Initiate an imaging study to get a presigned upload envelope, upload the file (single PUT or multipart for DICOM/CBCT) directly to storage, then finalize the study so it appears in the patient image library.

nodes.name: Cephalometric Tracing & Report
nodes.summary: Place/auto-detect landmarks on a ceph image, compute angle analysis, optionally superimpose against a prior tracing, and generate a version-pinned ceph report.

nodes.name: Present & Accept Treatment Plan
nodes.summary: Assemble a case presentation from a treatment plan and imaging, present it to the patient, and capture their e-signed acceptance or rejection as a legal artifact.

nodes.name: Invoice & Collect Payment
nodes.summary: Build an invoice from treatments, optionally apply a discount, issue it, record a payment, and produce a receipt — the core revenue-cycle journey.

nodes.name: Submit & Reconcile Insurance Claim
nodes.summary: Create an insurance claim with claim lines, estimate coverage, track status through the claims worklist, and record remittance against the invoice.

nodes.name: Payment Plans & Collections
nodes.summary: Set up a patient payment plan, monitor AR aging and the collections view, generate statement batches, and mark uncollectible balances.

nodes.name: Clinic Onboarding
nodes.summary: Multi-step wizard that bootstraps a new clinic: organization/clinic details, the first dentist, fee schedule defaults, and a first patient, then activates the organization.

nodes.name: PIN Chairside Authentication
nodes.summary: Staff select their member identity and enter a PIN to establish a chairside session that gates clinical workspace actions.

nodes.name: Manage Staff & Permissions
nodes.summary: Owner manages staff membership and the role-based permission grid, and resets member PINs from the staff admin screen.

nodes.name: Patient Portal Access
nodes.summary: Authenticated patient views their own upcoming appointments, invoices, and outstanding balance through the portal.

nodes.name: Notifications & Recalls
nodes.summary: Patients opt into push, receive routed notifications, and recall-due patients are surfaced for re-engagement scheduling.

nodes.name: Open registration modal
nodes.summary: Staff open the Patient Registration modal from the patients list to enter demographics.

nodes.name: Surface possible duplicates
nodes.summary: As demographics are entered, candidate duplicate patients are surfaced to prevent creating a redundant record.

nodes.name: Create patient record
nodes.summary: Submit the form to create the patient via the dental-patient identity handler.

nodes.name: Invalidate & refresh patient list
nodes.summary: On success the patients query is invalidated so the new patient appears.

nodes.name: List duplicate candidates
nodes.summary: The duplicate panel loads candidate matches for the current patient.

nodes.name: Review matches
nodes.summary: Staff review demographic overlap to decide whether records are the same person.

nodes.name: Merge patient records
nodes.summary: Confirmed duplicates are merged into a single canonical patient via the identity handler.

nodes.name: Open appointment modal
nodes.summary: Staff open the appointment modal from the calendar and pick patient, provider, and time.

nodes.name: Resolve availability & working hours
nodes.summary: Availability is computed against configured working hours before the slot is offered.

nodes.name: Create appointment
nodes.summary: POST creates the appointment in the scheduled state.

nodes.name: Confirm appointment
nodes.summary: Staff confirm the appointment (scheduled -> confirmed) via the dedicated confirm endpoint.

nodes.name: Check in appointment
nodes.summary: Staff check the patient in from the calendar, advancing the appointment FSM.

nodes.name: Enter chairside queue
nodes.summary: Check-in creates/places a queue item; the queue board renders waiting patients.

nodes.name: View & advance queue board
nodes.summary: Staff view the queue board and advance item status as the patient moves through the visit.

nodes.name: Choose service & provider
nodes.summary: Public booking wizard step 1: select visit type and provider.

nodes.name: Load live availability
nodes.summary: Fetch public availability for the chosen service to render the slot grid.

nodes.name: Hold a slot
nodes.summary: Selecting a slot creates a short-lived booking hold to prevent double-booking.

nodes.name: Enter contact details
nodes.summary: Patient enters contact details in the wizard details step.

nodes.name: Confirm booking
nodes.summary: Submit the held slot to create the online booking; a confirmation code is returned (rate-limited).

nodes.name: Click 'New Visit' (gated)
nodes.summary: Workspace New Visit action; gated by the one-open-visit rule computed from the visit list.

nodes.name: POST create draft visit
nodes.summary: Frontend step 1: createDentalVisit creates the visit in draft state.

nodes.name: PATCH draft -> active
nodes.summary: Frontend step 2: immediately updateDentalVisit to status='active' so the visit is chartable and completable (draft->completed is invalid).

nodes.name: Refresh visit timeline
nodes.summary: Invalidate listDentalVisits so the timeline shows the active visit.

nodes.name: Select tooth & surfaces
nodes.summary: Clinician selects a tooth and surfaces on the dental chart.

nodes.name: Record finding
nodes.summary: Record a clinical finding for the selected tooth/surface.

nodes.name: Convert finding to treatment
nodes.summary: Convert a finding into a proposed treatment on the plan.

nodes.name: Save chart (baseline merge)
nodes.summary: Persist chart tooth data; the save merges against a baseline and captures offline conflicts.

nodes.name: Resolve chart conflict
nodes.summary: If an offline conflict was persisted, the conflict banner lets the clinician resolve it.

nodes.name: Create perio chart
nodes.summary: Open a new periodontal chart for the visit.

nodes.name: Record probing readings
nodes.summary: Upsert per-tooth probing depth / BOP readings as the clinician probes.

nodes.name: Complete perio chart
nodes.summary: Complete the chart to lock staging; runs inside a transaction.

nodes.name: Compare with history
nodes.summary: Toggle the current exam against prior exams to show longitudinal trend.

nodes.name: Review medical history
nodes.summary: Pre-completion checklist requires the patient's medical history be reviewed.

nodes.name: Capture consent
nodes.summary: Capture required consent (or record a refusal) before completing.

nodes.name: Mark treatments done
nodes.summary: Clinician marks performed treatments complete.

nodes.name: PATCH active -> completed
nodes.summary: Transition the active visit to completed via updateDentalVisit.

nodes.name: Initiate study & get upload URL
nodes.summary: Create the imaging study and receive the presigned upload envelope (single PUT or multipart).

nodes.name: Upload binary to storage
nodes.summary: Frontend PUTs the file (or each multipart part) directly to S3/MinIO presigned URLs, bypassing the auth cookie.

nodes.name: Finalize study
nodes.summary: Finalize the study (e.g. CBCT) so it appears in the library.

nodes.name: View patient image library
nodes.summary: Uploaded study appears in the patient image list / imaging workspace.

nodes.name: Place / detect landmarks
nodes.summary: Place landmarks manually or auto-detect, then batch-upsert them.

nodes.name: Compute angle analysis
nodes.summary: Compute cephalometric angle analysis from the landmarks.

nodes.name: Superimpose tracings
nodes.summary: Optionally superimpose against a prior tracing to show growth/change.

nodes.name: Generate ceph report
nodes.summary: Generate a version-pinned ceph report (analysis_type/norm_version/formula_version pinned).

nodes.name: Assemble presentation
nodes.summary: Aggregate the treatment plan and imaging refs into a case presentation.

nodes.name: Present to patient
nodes.summary: Render the case presentation view to the patient at the chair.

nodes.name: Capture e-signed decision
nodes.summary: Patient accepts (e-signature) or rejects; the signed decision is persisted as a legal artifact.

nodes.name: View accepted plan
nodes.summary: The accepted plan (with signer artifact) is shown read-only.

nodes.name: Create invoice from treatments
nodes.summary: Build a draft invoice from the visit's treatments.

nodes.name: Apply discount (optional)
nodes.summary: Optionally apply a discount to the invoice.

nodes.name: Issue invoice
nodes.summary: Issue the invoice (draft -> issued) from the invoice detail view.

nodes.name: Record payment
nodes.summary: Record a patient payment against the issued invoice.

nodes.name: Produce receipt
nodes.summary: Generate the payment receipt for the patient.

nodes.name: Create insurance claim
nodes.summary: Create a claim with claim lines from billable treatments.

nodes.name: Estimate coverage
nodes.summary: Estimate payer coverage for the claim lines.

nodes.name: Track in claims worklist
nodes.summary: Monitor and update claim status from the claims worklist UI.

nodes.name: Record remittance
nodes.summary: Record payer remittance against the claim and invoice.

nodes.name: Create payment plan
nodes.summary: Set up an installment payment plan for the patient.

nodes.name: Review AR aging & collections
nodes.summary: Review accounts-receivable aging and the collections view (branch-scoped).

nodes.name: Generate statement batch
nodes.summary: Generate a batch of patient statements for outstanding balances.

nodes.name: Mark uncollectible
nodes.summary: Mark a balance uncollectible when collection is abandoned.

nodes.name: Enter clinic details
nodes.summary: Wizard step: organization/clinic name and details.

nodes.name: Add first dentist
nodes.summary: Wizard step: create the first dentist member.

nodes.name: Set fee schedule defaults
nodes.summary: Wizard step: configure default fee schedule entries.

nodes.name: Submit onboarding
nodes.summary: Persist the onboarding (org, member, fees, first patient) via createOnboarding.

nodes.name: Activate organization
nodes.summary: Activate the organization so product features unlock.

nodes.name: Select member
nodes.summary: Staff pick their identity from the PIN select screen.

nodes.name: Enter PIN
nodes.summary: Staff enter their PIN to establish a chairside session.

nodes.name: Verify PIN
nodes.summary: Backend verifies the PIN against the member record.

nodes.name: Establish PIN session
nodes.summary: A PIN session is stored and gates workspace routes.

nodes.name: List staff members
nodes.summary: Owner views staff on the staff admin screen.

nodes.name: Edit permission grid
nodes.summary: Owner updates the role-based permission grid.

nodes.name: Reset member PIN
nodes.summary: Owner resets a member's PIN.

nodes.name: View my appointments
nodes.summary: Patient sees their own upcoming appointments in the portal.

nodes.name: View my invoices
nodes.summary: Patient sees their own invoices in the portal.

nodes.name: View my balance
nodes.summary: Patient sees their outstanding balance in the portal.

nodes.name: Opt into push
nodes.summary: Patient opts into push notifications (OneSignal external_id targeting).

nodes.name: Route notification
nodes.summary: Incoming notifications are routed to the correct destination/screen.

nodes.name: Surface recall-due patients
nodes.summary: Recall-due patients are surfaced for re-engagement scheduling.

edges.description: Scheduling resolves patients and pulls recall-due patients from patient-management.
Appointment check-in feeds the patient into the clinical queue/workspace.
Every visit resolves and reads the patient record before charting.
Clinical findings and visits link to imaging studies and measurements.
Treatment plans built in the workspace are presented for patient acceptance.
Completed treatments become billable invoice line items.
Imaging studies are referenced in the case presentation.
Patient balance and insurance context are read from patient-management.
Fee schedules from org setup price billing invoices.
PIN sessions and RBAC gate clinical workspace actions; consent templates feed consent capture.
Appointment events trigger notifications and the portal reads appointments.
Payment/invoice events drive notifications; the portal exposes invoices and balance.
